title: phegopteris lineata
intro: Phegopteris lineata, also known as the Golden Spleenwort, is a type of fern native to North America, recognized by its lance-shaped fronds and golden-brown hairs on the underside of its leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Phegopteris lineata include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. Phegopteris lineata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
